dialog.stepLabel: Jiyan: "There is something I wish to do with you today...."

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: There is something I wish to do with you today.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: A seed.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: This is the seed of a resilient type of flower, which I asked the Academy to help me breed years ago.

options.text: Did you plant all these flowers here?

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: From here, you can see all of Jinzhou city.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: I thought... those who died for Jinzhou would want to see how it's thriving now.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: This flower... We named it the Emortia.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Yes. The word represents both "departure" and "return."

options.text: So the departed soldiers can one day return home...

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Yes. I know there's nothing we can do to bring them back, but for us still living, we need something like this to hold on to.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}, for finally bringing them peace. Now, they no longer have to fight.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: And this is why I believe you should be the one to plant this seed here.
You accept the seed. Despite its size, it feels heavy in your hand.
Together with Jiyan, you plant it. Perhaps one day, you'll watch the sunset again, with the flower you planted by your side.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: We have prevented one Outbreak for the time being.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: But a greater danger remains lurking in the darkness... Our knowledge of it is severely lacking.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: I must act to prevent more deaths...

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Thank you for your concern.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Every Midnight Ranger is prepared to die on the battlefield any day. I know this all too well as their general in command.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Helping the souls of our fallen ones return to their homeland... This is one of the few ways I can honor their sacrifices.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: {PlayerName}, I have one more request.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: If one day I perish in battle and cannot return home... Could you please plant one of these flowers for me?

options.text: I will not let you die.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}.

dialog.stepLabel: Wenshu: "{PlayerName}, I heard you've returned to the city with Gener..."

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: {PlayerName}, I heard you've returned to the city with General Jiyan?

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Ah, what a relief. I am beyond happy to know you're all safe and sound.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: There is something I forgot to give you. Let's meet up somewhere when you have the time.

dialog.stepLabel: Mortefi: "...We're no longer seeing any unusual fluctuations now...."

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: ...We're no longer seeing any unusual fluctuations now.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Good job. You should have successfully prevented a potential TD Outbreak.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: As you requested, I compared the fluctuations with those of the previous Outbreaks, and I did spot some similarities.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Thank you, Mortefi.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: I'm not done yet. Thank me later if you must.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Though there are some similarities, they aren't identical. I'm no expert in this field, and I can't give you any definitive conclusions yet.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: This is the frequency sample I've recorded.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Frequency sample from a Sonoro Sphere... I'll deliver it to a colleague. It's in better hands with her.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: It's someone you know. Baizhi. She specializes in Ecoacoustic research, and the Sonoro Sphere is her field of expertise.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Yes... Whatever you do, please keep it safe.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: What now, you don't trust me?

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: I trust you as always, Mortefi. But this sample... It means a lot to me.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Rest assured. You should know how careful we are with our samples... Especially such a valuable one.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: This should be our wake-up call. The Waveworn Phenomena is more complicated than we thought.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: It's a wake-up call, and it may also hold the answers we've been searching for all these years.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: By the way, the Riverside Games people were looking for you, Jiyan. Probably wanted to discuss holding the event again at a later date.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Sure, I'll go talk to them once everything is settled here.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Now, if there's nothing else, I'm going back to my lab.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: {PlayerName}, there is a place I need to visit. Could you come with me?

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Thank you. I need to pick up something first. See you at Knell Square.

items.speaker: Jiyan
items.text: Sure. I'll be waiting for you at Knell Square.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Greetings, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: You see those fish? They look so happy in the safety of their pond.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Happiness and safety often come at a cost, whether it be for fish or people.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: And if we do not feel the weight of that price, someone else is carrying it for us.

options.text: What happened to that temporary team ten years ago?

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Ten years... How time flies. I coordinated the Riverside Games that year, too.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Dozens of teams competed that day, and the race lasted well into the afternoon... The sunset was so beautiful, like what we're seeing now... It was so tranquil.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: That team was thrown together last minute, and nobody thought they could win. They even stumbled during their first exchange.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: But then, the team surprised everyone with their final runner's expert Gulpuff handling and victorious finish. It was truly a race to remember.

options.text: They must have been overjoyed to win.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Yes. I still remember how excited those kids looked...

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: Just like how you looked in this photo.

options.text: Is this a group photo of us?

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: We were worried. We weren't going to get a photo of you all together... Luckily, the photographer managed to capture a shot of the whole team.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: I should thank you for bringing that team together, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Wenshu
items.text: I'm planning to talk to Jiyan about hosting the Riverside Games another time. If all goes well, please join us!
